title: DesignDoc
description: Canonical DesignDoc schema. Source of truth for the Architect Agent output and Developer Agent input. Any change here propagates to both.

issue.description: GitHub issue number this DesignDoc was derived from

workItemTitle.description: Title from the WorkItem for traceability

approach.description: One paragraph describing the implementation strategy and why it fits existing patterns

filesRead.description: Every file examined during Architect analysis, including compiler-probe-surfaced files. Makes analysis scope auditable at Gate 1.

commitPlan.description: Ordered sequence of commits. Each must compile and pass all existing tests when applied in isolation.

order.description: Execution order — Developer applies these sequentially, never reordered

type.description: preparatory_refactor: behavior-preserving change enabling later commits. implementation: new behavior plus its tests in one atomic commit.

description.description: What this commit achieves. Developer derives the commit message from this.

change.description: What specifically changes in this file

testRequired.description: Required when the commit introduces or changes behavior. Omit for purely structural commits with no behavior change.

assertionIntent.description: What this test proves about behavior

refactoringsInScope.description: Essential refactorings included in the commit plan. Each must be behavior-preserving.

justification.description: Why this is essential — what cannot be implemented correctly without it

behaviorChange.description: Must always be false — refactoring must not change observable behavior

cosmeticRefactoringNotes.description: Quality observations in affected files. Not acted on by Developer — visible at Gate 1 for human decision.

estimatedScope.description: xs = 1-2 commits, s = 3-5 commits, m = 6-10 commits. Exceeding m means WorkItem scope is too large — return to PO.

decisionLog.description: Key architectural decisions made during analysis with rationale

gateQuestion.description: Single sentence directing the human's attention to the highest-risk decision in this plan
